_comment: Pinned official releases for install-retro-tools.mjs. The installer is FAIL-CLOSED: it refuses to stage (or run) any download whose bytes don't match the checksum pinned here. A null checksum means 'not pinned yet' and the installer will refuse that tool until you paste a hand-verified hash — never silently trust a download. dosbox-x/86box were sha256-verified on 2026-06-12 against their official assets; godot is verified against the publisher's SHA512-SUMS.txt (4.3-stable); qemu must be pinned by hand (its host blocks automated mirrors). To re-pin: run `node scripts/install-retro-tools.mjs --pin <tool>`, cross-check the printed hash against the project's OWN published checksums, then paste it here in a deliberate commit.

86box.userMustProvide: The official 86Box machine ROM set (github.com/86Box/roms) — the installer stages the PROGRAM only and never fetches ROM files.

qemu.thirdPartyNote: QEMU publishes no official Windows binary; qemu.org links to maintainer Stefan Weil's builds at qemu.weilnetz.de. There is NO trustworthy automated pin path here (the host blocks mirrors, and a sibling .sha512 from the same origin proves nothing if that origin is compromised). To enable QEMU: download a specific qemu-w64-setup-<ver>.exe from the URL above in a browser, verify it against Stefan Weil's published checksum, then set BOTH `installer` (exact filename) and `sha512` (the verified hash) here. Until then the installer refuses QEMU. Use --no-qemu to skip.

godot.note: Optional (--with-godot): the editor (~55 MB) + export templates (~900 MB!). Verified against the publisher's SHA512-SUMS.txt for 4.3-stable.
